steps.title: Update package.json — add @caistech/portfolio-gate ^0.2.0 (devDependencies) + add / upgrade @caistech/corporate-components to ^0.2.0 (dependencies)
steps.description:
Single package.json rewrite. add @caistech/portfolio-gate ^0.2.0 (devDependencies). add / upgrade @caistech/corporate-components to ^0.2.0 (dependencies).

Portfolio-gate (R13) brings the CI smoke tests, errorResponse helper, and static audits. Corporate-components (R1) ships <AuthForm/> in 0.2.0 — required for the R1 swap migration.

steps.title: Scaffold routes.config.json
steps.description: Default top-level route list (homepage, /pricing, /about, /contact, /login, /signup, /forgot-password, /privacy, /terms, /api/health). Edit to match your product's actual routes before running the smoke test.

steps.title: Scaffold auth.config.json
steps.description: Per-product auth path map used by portfolio-gate-smoke-auth. The four legs are wired against the conventional paths (/login, /signup, /forgot-password, /api/auth/*) — edit if your product diverges.

steps.title: Scaffold .github/workflows/gate.yml
steps.description: GitHub Action template — runs typecheck + lint + build + route + auth smoke tests on PR + push to main. Requires GITHUB_PACKAGES_TOKEN secret and PORTFOLIO_GATE_PREVIEW_URL repo variable.

files.content:
# Portfolio Standard gate — generated by @caistech/portfolio-migrator
#
# Runs typecheck + lint + build, then route + auth smoke tests against the
# preview deploy URL. Fails the action (and blocks Vercel promotion) on any
# non-zero exit.
#
# Closes Portfolio Standard:
#   R1  — Auth four-leg pattern (auth smoke step)
#   R10 — No verbatim Postgres errors (errorResponse helper, enforced at code level)
#   R13 — Route smoke test on every deploy (route smoke step)

name: portfolio-gate

on:
  pull_request:
    branches: [main, master]
  push:
    branches: [main, master]

jobs:
  gate:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 15

    env:
      PORTFOLIO_GATE_PREVIEW_URL: ${{ vars.PORTFOLIO_GATE_PREVIEW_URL }}

    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: lts/*
          cache: npm

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          if [ -f pnpm-lock.yaml ]; then
            corepack enable
            pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
          elif [ -f package-lock.json ]; then
            npm ci
          else
            npm install
          fi
        env:
          GITHUB_PACKAGES_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_PACKAGES_TOKEN }}

      - name: Typecheck
        run: npm run typecheck --if-present

      - name: Lint
        run: npm run lint --if-present

      - name: Build
        run: npm run build --if-present

      - name: Verify preview URL is set
        run: |
          if [ -z "$PORTFOLIO_GATE_PREVIEW_URL" ]; then
            echo "::error::PORTFOLIO_GATE_PREVIEW_URL is not set. Wire it via repo vars or a preview-deploy step."
            exit 1
          fi
          echo "Preview URL: $PORTFOLIO_GATE_PREVIEW_URL"

      - name: Route smoke test (Portfolio Standard R13)
        run: |
          npx --no-install portfolio-gate-smoke-routes \
            --config routes.config.json \
            --base-url "$PORTFOLIO_GATE_PREVIEW_URL"

      - name: Auth smoke test (Portfolio Standard R1/R4)
        run: |
          npx --no-install portfolio-gate-smoke-auth \
            --config auth.config.json \
            --base-url "$PORTFOLIO_GATE_PREVIEW_URL"

steps.title: Scrub vendor identity references
steps.description: Replace literal references to operator handle / mobile / Calendly / email with process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_VENDOR_* references. Marked NOTE because the exact substitution depends on the call site — string template vs JSX text vs prop value all need slightly different syntax. Review each before applying.
steps.body:
4 occurrences of vendor identity strings were detected. Replace each with a process.env reference and add the placeholder to .env.example.

| File | Find | Replace with |
|---|---|---|
| `src/components/corporate/CorporateFooter.tsx` | `mcmdennis` | `${process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_VENDOR_HANDLE ?? ''}` |
| `src/components/corporate/CorporateFooter.tsx` | `+61402612471` | `${process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_VENDOR_PHONE ?? ''}` |
| `src/components/corporate/CorporateFooter.tsx` | `calendly.com/mcmdennis` | `${process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_VENDOR_CALENDLY ?? ''}` |
| `src/components/corporate/CorporateFooter.tsx` | `dennis@corporateaisolutions` | `${process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_VENDOR_EMAIL ?? ''}` |

These are NOT auto-applied because the exact substitution depends on the call site:
- Inside a string template: `\${process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_VENDOR_EMAIL ?? ''}`
- Inside JSX text: `{process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_VENDOR_EMAIL}`
- Inside a prop value: `vendorEmail={process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_VENDOR_EMAIL}`

Apply by hand, verify with `npx portfolio-gate-audit-vendor-leak`, then commit.

files.content:
# Environment Variables
# Copy to .env.local and fill in values

# Supabase
NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=
NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL=
PLATFORM_TRUST_SUPABASE_URL=
SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY=

# AI / API Keys
ADMIN_API_KEY=
DATAWIZZ_API_KEY=
ELEVENLABS_API_KEY=
NEXT_PUBLIC_ELEVENLABS_API_KEY=

# Stripe
STRIPE_PRICE_FREE_MONTHLY=
STRIPE_PRICE_PREMIUM_MONTHLY=
STRIPE_PRICE_PREMIUM_YEARLY=
STRIPE_PRICE_STANDARD_MONTHLY=
STRIPE_PRICE_STANDARD_YEARLY=
STRIPE_SECRET_KEY=
STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET=

# Platform Trust
PLATFORM_TRUST_PROJECT_ID=
PLATFORM_TRUST_SERVICE_KEY=

# App
ADMIN_DASHBOARD_URL=
APP_URL=
DATAWIZZ_BASE_URL=
NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL=
NEXT_PUBLIC_PROPERTY_SERVICES_URL=

# Other
ABR_GUID=
DATAWIZZ_MODEL=
ELEVENLABS_VOICE_ID=
NEXT_PUBLIC_ELEVENLABS_AGENT_ASSESSMENT=
NEXT_PUBLIC_ELEVENLABS_AGENT_BASICS=
NEXT_PUBLIC_ELEVENLABS_AGENT_DERISK=
NEXT_PUBLIC_ELEVENLABS_AGENT_FINANCIAL=
NEXT_PUBLIC_ELEVENLABS_AGENT_PROPERTY=
NEXT_PUBLIC_ELEVENLABS_AGENT_SETUP=
NEXT_PUBLIC_MAPBOX_TOKEN=
NEXT_PUBLIC_PROPERTY_SERVICES_ANON_KEY=

# Portfolio Standard R11 — vendor identity routed through env vars.
# Replace the placeholders with the consuming brand's identity, or leave
# them as-is for a vendor-neutral white-label deploy.
NEXT_PUBLIC_VENDOR_NAME="Your Brand Here"
NEXT_PUBLIC_VENDOR_EMAIL="hello@yourbrand.com"
NEXT_PUBLIC_VENDOR_PHONE=""
NEXT_PUBLIC_VENDOR_CALENDLY=""
NEXT_PUBLIC_VENDOR_INSTAGRAM=""

# Portfolio Standard R6 — verified Resend subdomain (never the bare
# corporateaisolutions.com — not verified, silently fails delivery).
RESEND_FROM_EMAIL=noreply@updates.corporateaisolutions.com
